ucs.title: [LLM] TG-1314 mass tooling push via abused enterprise deployment agents (SCCM/Altiris/Kaseya/LANDesk/Tanium)
ucs.description: Hunts the signature TG-1314 (G0028) chain: stolen privileged domain credentials reused inside an enterprise software-deployment system (SCCM, Altiris, Kaseya, LANDesk/Ivanti, Tanium) to push interpreters or LOLBins to many endpoints at once. Detects when a software-deployment agent process spawns cmd/PowerShell/wscript/mshta/rundll32 carrying enumeration, download, or admin-share commands — the exact 'compromised-creds → deployment-tool abuse → cmd.exe' pattern Dell SecureWorks documented for TG-1314.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as child_procs dc(Processes.dest) as dest_count values(Processes.dest) as dests from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("ccmexec.exe","scnotification.exe","cmrcservice.exe","aexnsagent.exe","aexagentutil.exe","aexsvc.exe","agentmon.exe","kaseya.agentendpoint.exe","lddc.exe","landesk.managementsuite.coreservices.exe","vulscan.exe","tanium.exe","taniumclient.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe") by Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.user Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(process,"(?i)(net\s+(use|view|group|localgroup)|whoami|tasklist|quser|systeminfo|ipconfig\s+/all|DownloadString|Invoke-WebRequest|Invoke-Expression|-Enc(odedCommand)?\b|FromBase64String|certutil\s+-urlcache|bitsadmin\s+/transfer|copy\s+\\\\|xcopy\s+\\\\|\\\\[A-Za-z0-9._-]+\\(C\$|ADMIN\$|IPC\$))") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// TG-1314 — deployment-agent driven mass execution
let _deploy_agents = dynamic([
    "ccmexec.exe","scnotification.exe","cmrcservice.exe",            // Microsoft SCCM / MECM
    "aexnsagent.exe","aexagentutil.exe","aexsvc.exe",                  // Symantec Altiris
    "agentmon.exe","kaseya.agentendpoint.exe",                          // Kaseya
    "lddc.exe","landesk.managementsuite.coreservices.exe","vulscan.exe",// LANDesk / Ivanti
    "tanium.exe","taniumclient.exe"                                     // Tanium
  ]);
let _suspect_children = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe",
    "mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe"
  ]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_deploy_agents)
| where FileName in~ (_suspect_children)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"                                         // strip machine accounts
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(net\s+(use|view|group|localgroup)|whoami|tasklist|quser|systeminfo|ipconfig\s+/all|DownloadString|Invoke-WebRequest|Invoke-Expression|-Enc(odedCommand)?\b|FromBase64String|certutil\s+-urlcache|bitsadmin\s+/transfer|\\\\[A-Za-z0-9._-]+\\(C\$|ADMIN\$|IPC\$))"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          DeployAgent     = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          DeployAgentPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          DeployAgentCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          DeployAgentUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          ChildProc       = FileName,
          ChildCmd        = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildIntegrity  = ProcessIntegrityLevel,
          SHA256
// fan-out: same agent-driven child seen across many hosts in a short window = TG-1314 mass-push tell
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            HostsHit  = dcount(DeviceName),
            Hosts     = make_set(DeviceName, 50),
            CmdSamples= make_set(ChildCmd, 25),
            UsersUsed = make_set(strcat(AccountDomain, "\\", AccountName), 10)
            by DeployAgent, ChildProc
| where HostsHit >= 3                                                     // 3+ hosts in 7d on the same agent->child = anomalous push
| order by HostsHit desc, FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// TG-1314 — deployment-agent driven mass execution (SecurityEvent 4688)
let _deploy_agents = dynamic([
    "ccmexec.exe","scnotification.exe","cmrcservice.exe",
    "aexnsagent.exe","aexagentutil.exe","aexsvc.exe",
    "agentmon.exe","kaseya.agentendpoint.exe",
    "lddc.exe","landesk.managementsuite.coreservices.exe","vulscan.exe",
    "tanium.exe","taniumclient.exe"
  ]);
let _suspect_children = dynamic([
    "\\cmd.exe","\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe","\\wscript.exe","\\cscript.exe",
    "\\mshta.exe","\\rundll32.exe","\\regsvr32.exe","\\bitsadmin.exe","\\certutil.exe"
  ]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (_deploy_agents)
| where NewProcessName has_any (_suspect_children)
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(net\s+(use|view|group|localgroup)|whoami|tasklist|quser|systeminfo|ipconfig\s+/all|DownloadString|Invoke-WebRequest|Invoke-Expression|-Enc(odedCommand)?\b|FromBase64String|certutil\s+-urlcache|bitsadmin\s+/transfer|\\\\[A-Za-z0-9._-]+\\(C\$|ADMIN\$|IPC\$))"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, AccountDomain,
          DeployAgent = ParentLeaf, DeployAgentPath = ParentProcessName,
          ChildProc   = NewProcessName, ChildCmd = CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            HostsHit  = dcount(Computer),
            Hosts     = make_set(Computer, 50),
            CmdSamples= make_set(ChildCmd, 25),
            UsersUsed = make_set(Account, 10)
            by DeployAgent, ChildProc
| where HostsHit >= 3
| order by HostsHit desc, FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: TG-1314 Software Deployment Agent Spawns Interpreter With Recon Or Lateral Movement Commands
id: 7d3e1b94-0c0e-4d11-9a8a-tg1314deploya
status: experimental
description: Detects an enterprise software-deployment agent (SCCM/Altiris/Kaseya/LANDesk/Tanium) spawning cmd.exe, PowerShell, or scripting LOLBins with recon, download, or admin-share commands. TG-1314 (G0028) is documented to use stolen domain credentials inside such deployment systems to push tooling at scale.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0028/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1072
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.t1078.002
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_deploy_agent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\ccmexec.exe'
      - '\scnotification.exe'
      - '\cmrcservice.exe'
      - '\aexnsagent.exe'
      - '\aexagentutil.exe'
      - '\aexsvc.exe'
      - '\agentmon.exe'
      - '\kaseya.agentendpoint.exe'
      - '\lddc.exe'
      - '\landesk.managementsuite.coreservices.exe'
      - '\vulscan.exe'
      - '\tanium.exe'
      - '\taniumclient.exe'
  child_interpreter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
  suspicious_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'net use'
      - 'net view'
      - 'net group'
      - 'net localgroup'
      - 'whoami'
      - 'tasklist'
      - 'quser'
      - 'systeminfo'
      - 'ipconfig /all'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'Invoke-WebRequest'
      - 'Invoke-Expression'
      - '-EncodedCommand'
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - 'certutil -urlcache'
      - 'bitsadmin /transfer'
      - 'C$'
      - 'ADMIN$'
      - 'IPC$'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: parent_deploy_agent and child_interpreter and suspicious_cmd and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate scripted package deployments — baseline per agent and known package GUIDs.
level: high
ucs.rationale: TG-1314 is one of the very few tracked clusters whose tradecraft Dell SecureWorks pinned to *enterprise software-deployment system abuse* (T1072) using stolen domain credentials, rather than custom malware or zero-days. Generic 'parent spawns powershell' detections miss this because the parent is a trusted, signed management agent. Pinning the detection to the specific agent binaries (CCMExec, AeX
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] TG-1314 single-credential SMB fan-out from compromised remote-access infrastructure
ucs.description: Catches TG-1314's documented entry behaviour: using compromised credentials to log into remote-access infrastructure and then pivoting to many internal hosts over SMB/admin shares with the same domain account in a short window. One human-style domain account producing >=10 distinct destination hosts via Network logon from a single source within an hour is the TG-1314 lateral fan-out shape.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime dc(Authentication.dest) as dest_count values(Authentication.dest) as dests values(Authentication.src) as srcs from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=success Authentication.authentication_method IN ("NTLM","Kerberos") Authentication.signature_id IN ("4624","4769") AND NOT (Authentication.user="*$" OR Authentication.user IN ("SYSTEM","ANONYMOUS LOGON","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE")) by Authentication.user Authentication.src _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)` | where dest_count >= 10 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// TG-1314 — one domain user fans out over SMB to many hosts from a single source in <=1h
DeviceLogonEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where ActionType == "LogonSuccess"
| where LogonType == "Network"                              // SMB / admin-share access
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","anonymous logon","local service","network service","dwm")
| where Protocol in ("NTLM","Kerberos")
| where isnotempty(RemoteIP)
| where RemoteIPType in ("Private","Public")
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            DestCount = dcount(DeviceName),
            Destinations = make_set(DeviceName, 50),
            UsedNTLM = countif(Protocol == "NTLM"),
            UsedKerberos = countif(Protocol == "Kerberos")
            by AccountDomain, AccountName, RemoteIP, bin(Timestamp, 1h)
| where DestCount >= 10                                     // sustained fan-out from one source / one cred
| extend WindowMinutes = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| order by DestCount desc, FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// TG-1314 — one domain user fans out over SMB to many hosts from a single source
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| where EventID == 4624
| where LogonType == 3                                       // Network logon (SMB / admin shares)
| where AccountType == "User"
| where TargetUserName !endswith "$"
| where TargetUserName !in~ ("SYSTEM","ANONYMOUS LOGON","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE","DWM")
| where AuthenticationPackageName in ("NTLM","Kerberos")
| where isnotempty(IpAddress) and IpAddress !in ("-","::1","127.0.0.1")
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            DestCount = dcount(Computer),
            Destinations = make_set(Computer, 50),
            UsedNTLM = countif(AuthenticationPackageName == "NTLM"),
            UsedKerberos = countif(AuthenticationPackageName == "Kerberos")
            by TargetDomainName, TargetUserName, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where DestCount >= 10
| extend WindowMinutes = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| order by DestCount desc, FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: MITRE's G0028 entry is explicit that TG-1314 'used compromised credentials to log into a victim's remote access infrastructure' — i.e. the actor's identity-based pivot is the entire kill-chain. The fan-out aggregation (one user, one source IP, >=10 destinations within an hour, network logon type) is engineered to flag the pivot pattern itself rather than any single auth event, which fits an unattr
